reviews.review_title: Removed best part of the game
reviews.review_description: I bought this game back in 2020, and there was a scene where you shot yourself in the head. It was the most memorable part of the whole game, and the Devs took it out, which changed my owned copy. I do not want the devs ruining my copy that I own. Make a new game and stop ruining a game that people already own. I PAID for this, and they took content out without me having a choice. They should refund everyone who they forcefully removed content from the game.

This review used to be a 5 star. I’ve changed my mind due to the fact that (1. The game is stuck syncing and I can’t open it. (2. The recent removed self-harm scene drama. 

First of all, the syncing. I was playing beat saber and I check my apps, Superhot VR says it’s syncing. This worried me due to the fact that my friend had had this problem where it was syncing and it had been doing this for a few days. Him and I have no obvious way to fix this, so I’m stuck there. I’m also pretty sure this has occurred with other people as well. You can’t open it, and if you uninstall it and reinstall, you still can’t play. Also if you restart the headset, no dice.

Second of all, as of recent, the devs have decided to remove the self harm scenes in the main campaign. This, in my opinion, is a very questionable choice because there’s a button in the settings that disables the scenes in question. This also leaves gaps in the lore and story of the game. The first removed scene is you taking your in-game life with a pistol. This happens after the first few levels. This would portray your mind being uploaded to the system slowly, and is a Segway into the next part. The second scene is the player jumping off of a few stories high window, which portrays that the player is being addicted to this game and will do anything it says. From what I can tell, this is just a questionable choice from the developers, not part of a political movement or an attack of the “snowflakes”. This choice has been made after 4-5 years of this game being out, and in a game where you take the place of a (basically) hit man who takes out near 100 red dudes throughout the game. The game ends with you “destroying the pyramid” when after you grab a pyramid and the game ends. This shows that you have succumbed to the system and will play the game over and over for eternity. 

Third of all, I have been a fan of this series for years now, and I have 100% completed Superhot VR and Superhot. I love these games to death and this really has made take a look at this franchise and wonder, “Should I put my faith into a franchise that removes part of a game that millions have payed for, without any warning?” Do I think that the game should be review bombed? No, but that may be the bias talking. But, do I think this choice should be protested? Yes. This was a very dumb move by the devs.

So, reader, thank you for listening to my rambling on and on, have a nice day or evening.

It makes a game which already is great through its gimmick of "time moves when you move" and made everything physical. Dodging bullets, throwing knives/shuriken/katanas/etc, gun them into a salvo of smithereens and steamrolling the red guys with your bare hands have been infused into VR so it really feels like your the unstoppable force of the original. Another great thing is how easy it is to get back into; though you do need to complete the initial campaign to be able to choose modes like endless or to individually choose levels but that shouldn't be to hard to beat.

Now the issue that I have with this game (I believe a few other reviews pointed this out) is the fact that there is no joystick movement. This means you physically need to move and it means you need to have a medium/large area to play the full richness of SUPER HOT VR. Although it does somewhat up the physical immersion, it does not immerse me when you have to wiggle yourself or a controller to speed up red guys to get to you. That is the only reason I put it at 4/5 but this is overall one of the best games on quest and I would highly recommend it to new VR users.

Could be a bit longer, but other than that its great.

reviews.review_title: Wanna like it but it’s infuriating!
reviews.review_description:
Looks great and I would love to play it but the difficulty is unforgiving. I’ve beat Elden Ring but I cannot get past the third stage. You make one mistake, start over.  I honestly hate it cause it’s so amazing but when you punish the player cause they didn’t play it like you want it, it 
takes the fun out of it. Shame.

This game is not for the faint of heart. If you want a causal game that isn’t tiring then this isn’t the game for you, but for you hardcore shooter lovers that like a workout, this is where it’s at. I love how smooth the game is and how challenging it is. The fun doesn’t stop when you beat the game. There is also endless mode. This game will truly make you feel like John wick. And don’t even think about going to one star reviews, just buy the game.

It Has Been one of the Best VR Games since the platform became popular, not just because its a Good introduction to the world of VR action games, but it also has a unique mechanic centering around timing your ￼momentum to enemy me projectiles and creates an insane amount of Dopamine when you successfully do things quickly, I feel Like John Wick if he was made of Glass! Im going to try and See if I can get mods but I don’t think that will be easy…which is really the only blemish I have with this game and many others, I think it would be really awesome if the developers found a way to push that front, but for now I’m gonna push for the hard mode

This wouldve been a 5 star review if not for a certain part of the game being removed for censorship, still a great game though...
My review scale*
5- Amazing game, while still might not be perfect any flaws are forgivable, is well worth your time
4- Good game, might have a few minor issues or missed opportunities keeping it from being better, but is very playable and still an enjoyable experience
3- Mid tier game, not great but not awful, these games can go either way depending on who you are or what you want or expect out of the game
2- Not a very good game, but not completely broken or unplayable, you might really need to want to play this game to get any enjoyment out of it
1- Awful game, broken or unplayable, I don't see these games being for anyone unless significant updates fix the game

*This is an overly simplified review scale since meta does not allow for half stars so in some cases I may round a game up or down depending on a case by case scenario, 
my reviews are always honest and in no way swayed by anybody else and regardless if I received a game for free or not, these ratings and reviews are however part of a 
campaign for us as consumers to be better about reviewing games and helping bring the games we love the attention they deserve.

reviews.review_title: One of the best vr games
reviews.review_description:
Superhot VR is really fun and unique, with the cool mechanic where time only moves when you move. It feels awesome and immersive, but it’s a bit short and could use more levels.
But other than that it was SO fun.

The only interesting thing about it is the concept of defining the flow of time. Everything else is mundane, boring, and repetitive. Even someone who only cares for action will get bored in minutes. All enemies are red, all items are black, and none have distinctive sizes. not that I'm against poly graphics, but there's no variety whatsoever.
And all of this is coming from someone who beat the game. 

But the worst part about this is the fact that the last update was 4 years ago. This game is not worth your money.
Very peak game thought I was gonna get jumpscared when I inserted da disk into my my head (you dont)
